a-la-carte__10-business-posts.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What platforms do the posts work for?

We can format posts for Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, or whatever platform you use. Let us know where you’re posting and we’ll adjust the content accordingly.

Do I need to provide images?

Not required. If you have specific photos or brand assets you want used, send them over. Otherwise we’ll create or source appropriate visuals for each post.

How long does it take?

Typically 5-7 business days from when we receive your business details and any required assets.

Can I request revisions?

Yes. If a post doesn’t sound right or needs adjustment, let me know and we’ll revise it.

What if I need more than 10 posts?

We can do additional batches. Reach out and we’ll work out what makes sense for your posting schedule.
a-la-carte__10-business-posts.deliverables_month_1:
Deliverables

  • 10 custom social media posts
  • Written copy for each post
  • Graphics or images as needed
  • Content formatted for your chosen platforms
  • All files delivered ready to post
a-la-carte__10-business-posts.footer_inclusions: All posts include custom copy, platform-appropriate formatting, and graphics or images as needed.

a-la-carte__5-business-posts.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What platforms are these posts designed for?

Whatever you use. Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, I’ll format them to fit. If you need specific dimensions or formats, just let me know when you order.

Can I request specific topics or themes?

Yes. That’s the whole point. You tell me what you want to promote, and I’ll create posts around that. If you don’t have a specific direction, I’ll pull from your website and services to come up with something relevant.

Do I get the image files or just the final posts?

You get both. Final posts ready to upload, plus the source files (PSD, PNG, whatever format makes sense) so you can edit or repurpose them later.

How long does it take?

5 business days from the time you place the order and give me the direction. If I need more info, I’ll reach out.

Can I order more than one package?

Yes. If you need 10 posts, order two packages. If you need 15, order three. No limit.

Is this a subscription or a one-time payment?

One-time. You pay once, you get five posts, that’s it. If you want more later, you place another order.
a-la-carte__5-business-posts.footer_inclusions: Five custom social posts (copy + visuals), platform-ready formats, editable source files, 5-day turnaround.

a-la-carte__additional-gravity-forms.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What if I need multiple forms?

Order this add-on multiple times, or let me know upfront and I’ll quote you a bundle.

Can you connect the form to my CRM or email list?

Yes. Gravity Forms integrates with most major platforms (Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, Salesforce, Zapier, etc.). Let me know what you’re using and I’ll wire it up.

Do I need a Gravity Forms license?

If you don’t already have one, I’ll handle that as part of the setup. It’s included in the price.

Can I edit the form myself after you set it up?

Absolutely. Gravity Forms has a visual editor, you can add fields, change labels, tweak notifications, whatever you need. I’ll show you how if you want a walkthrough.
a-la-carte__additional-gravity-forms.footer_inclusions: One-time setup, configured and tested, ready to use.

a-la-carte__additional-pages.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many additional pages can I order?

As many as you want. Each one is $20. If you need 10 pages done, order 10. If you just need one, order one.

Is this a recurring charge?

No. One-time fee per page. Once it’s optimized, it’s done.

What if I need content written for the page?

That’s included. If the page is blank or needs a rewrite, we’ll handle it as part of the optimization. You’re not paying extra for copywriting here.

Does this include ongoing updates?

No. This is a one-time optimization. If you want the page monitored and updated over time, that’s part of your ongoing SEO package. This add-on just gets the page optimized once.

Can I add these to any SEO package?

Yes. Whether you’re on Local SEO, National SEO, or any other package, you can add extra pages whenever you need them.

a-la-carte__blog.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What if I need multiple articles?

You can order multiple one-time articles, or we can set up a monthly blog plan if you need consistent content. Monthly plans include 4 or 8 articles per month at a lower per-article rate.

Can I provide an outline or specific points to cover?

Yes. The more direction you give, the faster I can write it. If you have a rough outline, bullet points, or even just a general idea, send it over.

Do you write in my industry/niche?

I’ve written for local service businesses, retailers, B2B companies, and professional services in the Boise area. If your industry requires specialized knowledge, I’ll research it or ask you for background information.

What if I don’t like the first draft?

You get one revision round. I’ll adjust tone, add/remove sections, or rewrite parts based on your feedback. If the article misses the mark entirely, we’ll figure it out.

Do I own the content after I pay for it?

Yes. Once delivered and paid for, it’s yours. You can edit it, republish it, or do whatever you want with it.

Can you publish it directly to my website?

Not as part of this one-time service, but if you need help with that, let me know and we can handle it separately.
a-la-carte__blog.deliverables_month_1:
Delivered Once (No Monthly Billing)

  • Topic consultation and keyword research
  • 800-1200 word article, written and optimized
  • Meta title and description
  • One revision round
  • Final article delivered in editable format
a-la-carte__blog.footer_inclusions: Includes topic research, keyword optimization, one revision round, and delivery in 7-10 business days.

a-la-carte__call-tracking-local-number.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What kind of data do I see for each call?

Every call is logged with the campaign source, keyword (if PPC), landing page URL, caller location, time of day, call duration, and a full recording. All viewable in your dashboard.

Can I use this for offline marketing too?

Yes. You can assign tracking numbers to specific offline campaigns (print ads, mailers, billboards) to measure performance the same way you do for digital.

How long does setup take?

Under a minute. We configure the tracking number, point it to your business line, and it’s live.

What happens if I go over 200 minutes?

Additional minutes are billed at $0.09 each. You’ll see usage in your dashboard.

Do I need to change my business phone number?

No. Your business number stays the same. The tracking number forwards calls to it. Customers never see the difference.
a-la-carte__call-tracking-local-number.footer_inclusions: Local tracking number, up to 200 minutes per month, call recordings, campaign attribution, dashboard access

a-la-carte__call-tracking-toll-free-numb.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What’s included in the 200 minutes?

You get up to 200 inbound minutes per month at the base price. Additional minutes beyond that are billed at $0.14/minute.

Can I use this with my existing phone system?

Yes. The tracking number forwards to whatever number you specify. Your existing phone setup doesn’t change.

Do I own the tracking number?

The number is assigned to your account for as long as the service is active. If you cancel, the number goes back into the pool.

Can I see who called and where they came from?

Yes. Every call is logged with timestamp, duration, caller ID (if available), and the marketing source that drove it (PPC keyword, landing page URL, referral source, etc.).

Are call recordings automatic?

Recordings can be enabled per your preference. Check your local laws on call recording consent before turning this on.
a-la-carte__call-tracking-toll-free-numb.footer_inclusions: Toll-free number, call routing, source attribution, call logs, dashboard access, up to 200 minutes/month included.

a-la-carte__directory-submission.faqs_html:
What’s NAP and why does it matter?

NAP stands for Name, Address, Phone Number. It’s how search engines verify your business is legitimate. If your NAP is inconsistent across the web (different phone number formats, abbreviated vs. full street names, etc.), search engines don’t know which version to trust. Consistent NAP across citations is a ranking factor for local search.

Can I choose which directories you submit to?

I select directories based on relevance to your industry and location, plus their authority and trustworthiness. If you have specific directories in mind, let me know and I’ll include them where it makes sense.

How long does it take?

Most submissions are completed within 2-3 weeks. Some directories have manual review processes, so a few might take longer to go live. You’ll get the full report as soon as all submissions are processed.

What if my business information changes later?

Citation updates are a separate service. If your address, phone number, or business name changes, you’ll want to update these listings to maintain consistency. I can handle that as a follow-up project.

Do these citations include backlinks?

Yes. Most directory listings include a link back to your website. These are legitimate, relevant links from authoritative sources - exactly the kind of links that help your SEO.

Is this safe for my site?

Absolutely. These are manual submissions to legitimate directories, not automated spam. The directories we use are Google-friendly and focused on providing real value to users searching for local businesses.
a-la-carte__directory-submission.deliverables_month_1:
This is a one-time service. All 25 submissions are completed within the first delivery cycle:

  • Submission to 25 high-quality, relevant directories and citation platforms
  • Creation and optimization of listings with consistent NAP details
  • Keyword-aligned business descriptions for search and user engagement
  • Placement in relevant categories for local and contextual relevance
  • Focus on authoritative directories for long-term SEO value
  • Detailed report with submitted directories and live URLs
a-la-carte__directory-submission.footer_inclusions: Includes 25 directory submissions, optimized listings with consistent NAP, detailed completion report with live URLs.

a-la-carte__divi-builder.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need to buy a Divi license separately?

No. The license is included in this service. I handle the activation.

Can you build pages for me with Divi?

This service is installation only. If you want pages built, that’s a separate project. We can talk about it.

What if I need help using Divi after setup?

I’ll show you the basics during setup. For ongoing support or training, we can discuss a separate arrangement.

Will this work with my existing WordPress theme?

Divi can work as a plugin alongside most themes. If there are conflicts, I’ll let you know during setup.

How long does setup take?

Usually 1-2 business days from the time I get access to your WordPress admin.
a-la-carte__divi-builder.deliverables_month_1:
One-Time Setup

  • Install Divi Builder plugin on your WordPress site
  • Activate and register the Divi license
  • Configure basic theme settings
  • Set default layout preferences
  • Test builder functionality across desktop and mobile
  • Provide brief overview of how to use the builder
a-la-carte__divi-builder.footer_inclusions: Includes Divi license, installation, configuration, and basic walkthrough.

a-la-carte__guest-blog-post-writing-and-.faqs_html:
How long does it take?

5 to 7 business days from order to published post. That includes research, writing, approval, and placement.

Where does the post get published?

On a relevant, high-quality site in your niche. I handle placement. The site will have real traffic and editorial standards.

Do I get to review the content before it goes live?

Yes. I send you the draft for approval before publishing.

What if I need revisions?

I’ll make reasonable revisions to get the tone and messaging right. The goal is a post that represents your brand well.

Can I specify the target keyword or topic?

Absolutely. You give me the topic, target keyword, and tone. I take it from there.

Is the backlink dofollow?

Typically yes, but it depends on the site’s editorial policy. I prioritize placement quality over link attributes. A contextual link on a relevant, authoritative site has value either way.

How does this help with AI search?

The content is structured for how AI engines parse and surface information: clear entity signals, FAQ elements, natural language that answers search intent. It’s written for humans first, but formatted to perform in AI Overviews and answer boxes.

Can I order multiple posts?

Yes. Each order is for one post. If you want an ongoing campaign, just order multiple units or reach out to discuss a package.
a-la-carte__guest-blog-post-writing-and-.footer_inclusions: Full content creation, image sourcing, backlink placement, and publishing on a relevant, high-quality site.

a-la-carte__infographic-creation-and-dis.faqs_html:
What file formats do I get?

You’ll receive the infographic in multiple formats optimized for different uses - web, social, print-ready. We’ll also give you recommendations on where each format works best.

Do you handle the distribution?

We provide a distribution plan and guidance. You handle the actual posting and outreach, but we tell you exactly where to go and how to pitch it for backlinks and visibility.

Can I request revisions?

Yes. We include one round of revisions to make sure the final product matches your vision and goals.

How long does it take?

Typically 2-3 weeks from kickoff to final delivery. Research and data validation take time if we’re doing it right.

What if I don’t have data to visualize?

We can work with you to identify relevant industry data, survey results, or internal metrics that tell a story your audience cares about.

a-la-carte__keyword-research.faqs_html:
Why only 15 keywords?

Because focus works. Trying to rank for 100 keywords at once spreads your efforts too thin. Fifteen well-chosen keywords, executed properly, will outperform a scattered approach every time. If you need more later, we can add them.

What if I already know which keywords I want to target?

Perfect. Bring them. I’ll validate them against search data and competition levels, then either confirm they’re solid or suggest adjustments. Your input is critical here.

How do you choose which keywords to prioritize?

Three factors: search intent (are people looking to buy or just browsing?), competition level (can we realistically rank?), and alignment with your business goals (does it bring the right customers?). Volume matters, but it’s not the only thing that matters.

Can these keywords change later?

Yes. Keyword strategy isn’t set in stone. Markets shift, search behavior changes, your business evolves. We can revisit and adjust as needed.

a-la-carte__logo-designing.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many revisions do I get?

Enough to get it right. This isn’t a ticket system where you run out of changes. We work through revisions until you’re happy with the result. That said, the process works best when you give clear feedback, tell me what’s working and what isn’t, and we’ll get there faster.

What if I don’t like any of the initial concepts?

Then we start over. If the first round misses the mark entirely, I’ll create new directions based on your feedback. The goal is a logo you actually want to use, not something you settle for.

Can you match a style I’ve seen somewhere else?

I can work in a similar direction, but I won’t copy another company’s logo. If you show me examples of styles you like, that helps me understand what you’re after. Think of it as inspiration, not duplication.

Do I need to provide anything before we start?

Just information. Business name, what you do, who your customers are, and any visual preferences. If you have existing brand colors or fonts you want to incorporate, share those. If you have examples of logos you like (or hate), that helps too.

What if I need the logo in a different format later?

The vector files I provide can be converted to basically any format you’ll ever need. If you run into a situation where you need something specific and can’t figure it out, reach out and I’ll export whatever you need.

Will the logo work in black and white?

Yes. I design logos to work in full color and in black and white (or single-color). You’ll get both versions in your final file package.

Can I trademark the logo?

You own it, so you can trademark it if you want. That’s a separate legal process (filing with the USPTO), not something I handle as part of the design service. Most small businesses don’t trademark their logos, but it’s an option if brand protection matters to you.
a-la-carte__logo-designing.footer_inclusions: Includes initial concepts, revision rounds, and final files in multiple formats (vector and web-ready). One-time fee, you own the logo outright.

a-la-carte__on-page-optimization.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

Is this a one-time service or ongoing?

One-time. We optimize the page once. If you need ongoing updates later (like refreshing content or adjusting for new keywords), that’s a separate project.

What if I have multiple pages that need optimization?

This service is per page. If you need multiple pages optimized, we can bundle them or run them as separate projects depending on scope.

Will I see results immediately?

Google needs time to re-crawl and re-index the page. Most clients see ranking improvements within 2-4 weeks, but it depends on competition and how often Google crawls your site.

Do you write the content or just optimize what’s already there?

We optimize existing content and provide recommendations for what to add or change. If you need full content writing, that’s a separate service.

What if my page is already ranking, will this hurt it?

No. We don’t make changes that would disrupt existing rankings. If something is already working, we leave it alone. We only fix what’s holding the page back.

Do I need to do anything after the optimization is complete?

Not unless we recommend content changes. If we suggest adding a section or rewriting a paragraph, you’ll need to implement that (or we can handle it as part of a content project).
a-la-carte__on-page-optimization.deliverables_month_1:
This is a one-time service. All work is completed within the project timeline:

  • Optimization of title tags and meta descriptions
  • Header structure (H1, H2, H3) refinement
  • Image alt tag optimization
  • Internal linking improvements
  • HTML, URL structure, and canonical tag cleanup
  • Schema markup implementation
  • Social profile link integration
  • Content recommendations based on search intent and semantic analysis
  • Mobile responsiveness check and adjustments
  • Full optimization report delivered
a-la-carte__on-page-optimization.footer_inclusions: Full optimization report with all changes documented

a-la-carte__photo-gallery.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many photos can I add?

The initial setup includes up to 50 images. After that, you can add as many as you want through the dashboard. If you need help with bulk uploads or organizing hundreds of images, we can quote that separately.

Can I organize photos into categories?

Yes. We can set up categories or albums (e.g., “Residential Projects”, “Commercial Work”). Let me know what structure makes sense for your business and I’ll configure it during setup.

What image formats do you support?

JPG, PNG, WebP. I’ll optimize whatever you upload for web performance, compressed file sizes, proper dimensions, all that.

Can I add captions or descriptions?

Yes. Every image gets a title field and a caption field. Use them or leave them blank.

Is this mobile-friendly?

Yes. The gallery adjusts to any screen size. Images load at the right resolution for the device, so mobile visitors get fast load times.

Can I use this for before/after comparisons?

Absolutely. We can set up side-by-side layouts or slider formats for before/after shots if that’s what you need.
a-la-carte__photo-gallery.deliverables_month_1:
One-Time Setup Includes

  • Gallery page creation and design integration
  • Mobile-responsive layout configuration
  • Image optimization for fast load times
  • Dashboard access for ongoing photo management
  • Initial photo upload (up to 50 images)
  • Caption and title fields for each image
  • SEO-friendly image file naming and alt text
a-la-carte__photo-gallery.footer_inclusions: One-time setup. Lifetime access to your gallery dashboard. Mobile-responsive design. Image optimization included.

a-la-carte__press-release-creation-and-d.faqs_html:
How is this different from just posting news on my website?

Posting on your site is good. But a press release goes further. It gets distributed to third-party news sites and PR networks. That creates backlinks, referral traffic, and credibility signals Google actually cares about. Plus, press releases show up in Google News and get picked up by AI tools answering questions about your industry.

What makes a good press release topic?

Anything genuinely newsworthy. New service launch. Office expansion. Award or certification. Community involvement. Industry milestone. Partnership announcement. If it’s something a local reporter or industry publication might actually care about, it works.

Do I need to write anything?

Nope. You give me the raw info (what happened, why it matters, relevant dates/details). I handle the writing. I’ll send you a draft for approval before we distribute.

Where does it get distributed?

We push it to PR distribution networks and relevant news/industry sites. You’ll get a report showing where it landed and any backlinks it generated.

Can I use this for ongoing announcements?

Yes. Some clients do one press release per quarter (new hire, service launch, seasonal push). Others do them as-needed. It’s a one-time project each time, so you control the cadence.
a-la-carte__press-release-creation-and-d.footer_inclusions: Includes professional writing, SEO optimization, AEO/GEO structuring, and full distribution to PR networks and news sites.

a-la-carte__qa-writing-and-posting.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

Why Quora specifically?

Quora has high domain authority and its answers rank well in Google. AI tools also pull from it. It’s a public forum where your answer has a long shelf life, and the platform’s structure makes it easy for search engines and AI to extract and display your content.

Will the backlink help my SEO?

Yes, but it’s one link. The value is in diversification and the type of signal it sends. It’s a contextual, user-generated link from a high-authority domain. That’s different from a blog comment or directory listing, and search engines recognize that.

How do you choose the question?

I look for questions that match your niche, have search volume or engagement, and where your site is a logical resource to reference. The question should be something your target customer would actually ask.

What if my answer gets removed?

I follow Quora’s guidelines to minimize that risk. If it does get removed (rare), let me know and I’ll repost with adjustments or find a different question.

Can I see the answer before it’s posted?

Yes. If you want approval before publishing, just note that in your order and I’ll send a draft. This may add a day or two to turnaround.

Do you use my business name or post anonymously?

I post from a credible account, not your personal profile. The answer references your business naturally in the context of the response, and the backlink goes to your site. I can discuss account strategy if you have specific preferences.
a-la-carte__qa-writing-and-posting.footer_inclusions: One researched, published Quora answer with a contextual backlink to your site.

a-la-carte__social-bookmarking.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What content do you submit?
You tell me which page or piece of content you want promoted. Could be your homepage, a service page, a blog post, whatever makes sense for your business right now.

How long does this take?
Usually completed within 7-10 business days. You’ll get the full report as soon as all submissions are live.

Are these links permanent?
Yes. These are one-way backlinks that stay up as long as the platform exists. No recurring fees to keep them active.

Will this hurt my rankings?
No. We only use quality platforms and follow search engine guidelines. This is a safe, white-hat approach to building links and visibility.

Can I order this multiple times?
Absolutely. You can order this for different pages or content pieces. Just space them out so it looks natural (not 100 submissions in one day).

a-la-carte__social-profiles-creation.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What exactly is social media marketing?

It’s using platforms like Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, or X to promote your business. You post content, text, images, videos, polls, whatever fits your brand, to reach people who might buy from you. Done right, it builds awareness and keeps you top-of-mind when someone needs what you sell.

Who should use this service?

Businesses that want a professional social presence but don’t have time to set it up themselves. If you’ve been meaning to get on Instagram or LinkedIn for six months and it still hasn’t happened, this gets it done.

What happens after I order?

You’ll hear from me within one business day. I’ll ask which platform you want, get your branding assets (logo, colors, any photos you want to use), and build the profile. Turnaround is typically 2-3 business days.

Which platforms can you set up?

Facebook, Instagram, X (Twitter), or LinkedIn. Pick one. If you want more than one profile, you’ll need to order this service multiple times.

Can I manage the account myself after you set it up?

Yes. You’ll have full admin access. The dashboard I give you makes it easy to post, schedule content, and track engagement. You can also connect the profile to other tools (Hootsuite, Buffer, etc.) if you prefer.

Will this guarantee more sales or leads?

No. A social profile is infrastructure, not a campaign. It’s like building a storefront, it needs to exist before people can walk in, but you still have to get them to the door. If you want active posting, engagement, and lead generation, that’s a separate service (Social Media Management).

Do I get to review the profile before it goes live?

Yes. I’ll send you a preview link and screenshots. You can request changes before I hand over the keys.

I don’t want to give you my personal Facebook password. How does this work?

You don’t have to. For Facebook and Instagram, you grant access through Business Manager, no password required. For LinkedIn and X, I’ll either set up the profile from scratch using a business email, or you can create it and add me as an admin temporarily. I never ask for personal login credentials.

What if I need help after the profile is live?

Reach me through the dashboard or email. If you have questions about posting, settings, or anything else, I’m available. This is a one-time setup fee, but I don’t disappear after delivery.

Can I cancel or get a refund?

If I haven’t started work yet, full refund. If I’m halfway through, partial refund based on work completed. Once the profile is delivered and you’ve approved it, the service is complete, no refunds at that point.
a-la-carte__social-profiles-creation.footer_inclusions: One social profile (Facebook, Instagram, X, or LinkedIn) • Full admin access • Dashboard login • Brand-aligned setup (logo, bio, imagery)

a-la-carte__video-ppt-creation-and-distr.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What kind of video are we talking about?

A short marketing video (typically 60, 90 seconds) using stock footage, text overlays, and your brand messaging. Think explainer video or service overview, not documentary or testimonial reel.

Do I need to be on camera?

No. This is built entirely from stock footage and graphics. If you want to be on camera, that’s a different project (and a different price).

What if I don’t have a script?

I write one based on what you tell me about your business. You review and approve before production.

Where does the video get distributed?

YouTube is the anchor platform. The presentation version goes to Slideshare. Additional distribution may include Vimeo, Dailymotion, or niche platforms depending on your industry.

Do I get the files?

Yes. You get the final video file and the presentation file. You can reuse them however you want, embed on your site, share on social, send in emails, etc.

How long does this take?

Typically 7, 10 business days from intake to final delivery, depending on revisions.

Is this a monthly service?

No. This is a one-time project. You pay once, you get the video and distribution once. If you want more videos later, we do another project.

Can I request revisions?

Yes, within reason. You get one round of revisions included. If you need major changes after approval, that’s a separate scope conversation.
a-la-carte__video-ppt-creation-and-distr.deliverables_month_1:
Project Deliverables (One-Time)

  • Intake call to define messaging, target audience, and key selling points
  • Script development based on your business and offer
  • Professional video creation using royalty-free stock footage (no custom filming)
  • Conversion of video to presentation format (slides with voiceover or text overlays)
  • Upload and optimization on YouTube (title, description, tags)
  • Upload and optimization on Slideshare or similar presentation-sharing platform
  • Distribution to additional video-sharing networks as applicable
  • Delivery of final video file (MP4 or similar) and presentation file (PPT/PDF) for your own use
  • Published links provided for all distribution channels
a-la-carte__video-ppt-creation-and-distr.footer_inclusions: Includes: Video production (stock footage), presentation conversion, distribution to YouTube and Slideshare, final file delivery, one round of revisions.

a-la-carte__visual-composer.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What if Visual Composer doesn’t work with my theme?

Most modern WordPress themes play nice with Visual Composer. If there’s a conflict, I’ll let you know during setup. We can either switch themes or troubleshoot the conflict depending on what’s causing it.

Do I need to know how to code?

No. That’s the whole point. Visual Composer is drag-and-drop. You pick elements, drop them on the page, and adjust settings in a panel. It’s more like using PowerPoint than writing HTML.

Is this a monthly service?

No. One-time setup fee. After that, Visual Composer is yours to use. The plugin itself has a license fee (separate, you pay that to Visual Composer directly), but my setup work is done once.

Can you build pages for me with Visual Composer after setup?

That’s a separate service. This package is just the install and configuration. If you want me to build specific pages or templates using Visual Composer, we can quote that separately.

What’s the difference between Visual Composer and Elementor?

Both are page builders. Visual Composer has a frontend and backend editor. Elementor is frontend-only. Some people prefer one interface over the other. If you already know which one you want, great. If not, I can give you a recommendation based on your site and workflow.
a-la-carte__visual-composer.deliverables_month_1:
One-Time Setup Includes

  • Visual Composer plugin installation on your WordPress site
  • License key activation
  • Basic configuration and settings optimization
  • Theme compatibility verification
  • Test page build to confirm functionality
  • Quick walkthrough of the interface (optional, 15 minutes)
a-la-carte__visual-composer.footer_inclusions: Setup includes plugin installation, license activation, configuration, theme compatibility check, and test page creation.

a-la-carte__voiceover-video-creation-and.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

How long is the video?

affiliate__basic-plan.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is affiliate marketing and how does it work?

Affiliate marketing is performance-based sales. You recruit third parties (affiliates) who promote your products. They only get paid when they generate a sale or lead. You’re paying for results, not exposure. Affiliates post your banners or links on their blogs, coupon sites, social feeds, etc. When a customer clicks through and buys, the network tracks it and pays the affiliate a commission you’ve set.

Who are affiliates and how do they get paid?

Affiliates can be individual bloggers, influencers, or companies running coupon sites, deal sites, loyalty programs, or content sites. They get paid per sale or per lead, depending on your program terms. The network handles all payouts automatically at the end of each month based on validated sales.

What are affiliate networks and why do I need them?

Networks are the platforms where affiliate programs live. They let affiliates register, host your banners and links, handle all tracking with cookies, and pay out commissions. Without a network, you’d have to build all that infrastructure yourself. Common networks: ShareASale, CJ, LinkConnector, Admitad, Linkshare. We manage accounts on all of these.

Is it necessary to create banners?

Yes. Banners are the primary tool affiliates use to promote you. You need a variety of sizes and formats. The more options you give affiliates, the more likely they are to promote your program. We update banners regularly based on performance.

How do I decide what commission to offer?

Commission is one of the main factors affiliates consider when choosing programs. You need to balance what your competitors offer, your profit margins, your conversion rate, and your brand strength. We’ll help you set a structure that attracts affiliates without killing your margins.

How do I pay for sales or leads?

All sales go to you for validation. You confirm or cancel them (in case an order was refunded or never paid). At the end of the month, the network sends you a list of validated sales. You only pay commission on sales you’ve confirmed and collected money for.

What is the tracking code?

It’s a script we place on your checkout thank-you page. It tells the network the order ID and amount for each sale so the network can attribute it to the right affiliate. Installation is straightforward. We provide the code, you (or your developer) drop it on the page.

What is a cookie and how long should it last?

A cookie is a small file saved on a user’s computer when they click an affiliate’s link. It tracks them for a set period (the cookie duration). If they buy within that window, the affiliate gets credit. Longer cookie durations are more attractive to affiliates. Example: 30-day cookie means if someone clicks today and buys 25 days later, the affiliate still gets paid. If they buy on day 32, the cookie has expired and the affiliate gets nothing.

When are we ready to start?

Once you’ve approved the program terms and we’ve installed the tracking code on your site, we’re live. I start recruiting affiliates immediately. They can begin promoting as soon as they’re approved.

affiliate__initial-plan.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is affiliate marketing and how does it benefit me?

Affiliate marketing is outsourced sales. You pay a commission only when someone buys. Affiliates, bloggers, coupon sites, influencers, promote your product to their audience. When their promotion leads to a sale, they get a cut. You’re paying for results, not clicks or impressions.

It works because affiliates have existing audiences. They’re not building traffic from scratch; they’re leveraging what they already have. You tap into that.

Who are affiliates and how do they get paid?

Affiliates are publishers: bloggers, content sites, coupon platforms, loyalty programs, deal aggregators, influencer networks. They get paid per sale or per lead, depending on your commission structure. The affiliate network handles all payouts automatically at the end of each month.

What are affiliate networks and why do I need one?

Affiliate networks are the infrastructure. They host your banners and links, track clicks and sales via cookies, generate reports, and pay affiliates. Without a network, you’d be managing all of that manually, tracking who sent what traffic, calculating commissions, cutting checks. The network automates it.

Common networks: ShareASale, CJ (Commission Junction), LinkConnector, Admitad, Rakuten (Linkshare), Pepperjam.

Which networks do you work with?

ShareASale, CJ, LinkConnector, Pepperjam, Admitad, Clixgalore, Commission Factory, Rakuten. If you’re already on one of these, I integrate with your existing account. If you’re starting fresh, I’ll recommend the best fit for your industry and help you get set up.

Do I need banners?

Yes. Banners are the primary promotional tool affiliates use. If you don’t provide them, most affiliates won’t bother promoting you, they’re not going to design assets themselves. You need a variety of sizes (leaderboard, skyscraper, square, etc.) and you should update them periodically to keep them fresh.

How do I decide what commission to offer?

Look at what your competitors are offering. Factor in your profit margins, conversion rate, and product price point. If you’re a recognized brand, you can offer slightly lower commissions. If you’re new, you’ll need to be competitive or above-market to attract affiliates.

I’ll walk you through this. We’ll look at comparable programs and land on a structure that attracts affiliates without eating your margins.

How do I pay commissions?

At the end of each month, the network sends you a report of validated sales. You review it, cancel any orders that were refunded or never paid for, and approve the rest. The network then pays affiliates automatically. You’re only paying commissions on sales where you’ve actually collected revenue.

What’s the tracking code and why do I need it?

It’s a small script that goes on your checkout thank-you page. It tells the affiliate network when a sale happened, the order ID, and the sale amount. Without it, the network can’t attribute sales to affiliates. Installation is straightforward, usually just copy-paste into your cart platform.

What’s a cookie and how long should it last?

A cookie is a tracking file saved on the user’s computer when they click an affiliate link. It tracks that user for a set period (the cookie duration). If they come back and buy within that window, the affiliate gets credit.

Example: 30-day cookie. User clicks an affiliate link today, doesn’t buy, comes back in 25 days and completes an order, affiliate gets credit. If they come back in 35 days, the cookie has expired, affiliate gets nothing.

Longer cookies are more attractive to affiliates. Industry standard is 30-60 days for most e-commerce.

Can I cancel anytime?

Yes. This is a monthly subscription. No long-term contract. If you cancel mid-month, we settle up based on work completed, could be a full refund, partial refund, or no refund depending on timing.

affiliate__one-time-setup.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is affiliate marketing and how does it work?

Affiliate marketing is paying a commission to third parties (affiliates) who drive sales or leads to your site. They promote your products on their blogs, coupon sites, social feeds, etc. When someone clicks their link and buys from you, they earn a percentage of that sale.

You’re paying for results. No sale, no commission. It’s lower-risk than traditional advertising because you only pay out after you’ve already collected revenue from the customer.

Who are affiliates?

Affiliates can be individual bloggers, influencers, or companies. Common types:

  • Coupon and deal sites
  • Content and review blogs
  • Loyalty and rewards platforms
  • Social media influencers

They get paid per sale or per lead, depending on how you structure your program. All payments go through the affiliate network, which acts as the middleman.

What are affiliate networks and why do I need one?

Affiliate networks (like ShareASale or CJ) are platforms that connect you with affiliates. They host your creative (banners, links), handle all tracking via cookies, generate reports, and process commission payments.

The network tracks a customer’s path from the affiliate’s site to your checkout page. If the customer buys, the network attributes the sale to the affiliate and calculates the commission based on your program rules.

You could run an affiliate program independently, but networks make it easier. They provide the infrastructure, the affiliate pool, and the trust layer between you and your partners.

Which networks do you set up on?

We work with ShareASale, CJ, LinkShare, LinkConnector, Pepperjam, Admitad, Clixgalore, and Commission Factory. We’ll recommend the best one based on your product category and where your target affiliates are active.

Do I need banners or just text links?

This package includes two text links. Banners help, especially if you want affiliates to place visual ads on their sites. Text links work well for content sites and blogs where contextual links perform better.

If you want a full set of banner creatives in multiple sizes, that’s a separate project. We can quote that separately.

How do I decide what commission to offer?

Commission structure depends on:

  • What your competitors are offering affiliates
  • Your profit margins
  • Your site’s conversion rate
  • Your product price point

We’ll help you figure out a commission rate that attracts affiliates but still makes sense for your business. Affiliates compare programs, so if your rate is too low compared to competitors, they’ll promote someone else.

How do I pay affiliates?

At the end of each month, you’ll see a list of sales in your dashboard. You validate or cancel each one (cancel if the order was refunded, never paid, etc.). Once you validate, the network processes commission payments to affiliates.

You’re only paying for sales you’ve actually collected money on. You approve first, then the network pays out.

What is the tracking code and where does it go?

The tracking code is a small script that goes on your checkout confirmation page (the “thank you” page after someone completes an order). It passes the order ID and sale amount to the network so the sale can be attributed to the affiliate who sent the customer.

It’s a simple install. We’ll provide the code and instructions. Your developer drops it on the confirmation page and passes two parameters: order ID and order total.

What is a cookie and how long should it last?

A cookie is a small file saved on the customer’s device when they click an affiliate link. It tracks that customer for a set period (the cookie duration).

If your cookie duration is 30 days and someone clicks an affiliate link today but doesn’t buy until 20 days later, the affiliate still gets credit for that sale. If they buy on day 35, the cookie has expired and the affiliate gets nothing.

Longer cookie durations are more attractive to affiliates. Standard is 30 days. Some programs go 60 or 90 days.

When can I start recruiting affiliates?

Once the tracking code is live and your program is set up on the network, you’re ready. Affiliates can start applying to your program immediately. You approve or decline them through your dashboard.

We handle the technical setup. You handle affiliate recruitment and approval. If you want help recruiting affiliates, that’s a separate ongoing service.

How do I get support after setup?

You work with me. Any questions about your affiliate program, dashboard, or network setup come through me. Campaign reports go to you through the dashboard. If something technical breaks, I loop in the team to fix it.

The network uses cookies to track a customer’s journey from the affiliate’s site through your checkout. Common networks include ShareASale, CJ (Commission Junction), LinkConnector, and Linkshare.

Which affiliate networks do you manage?

We manage ShareASale, Linkshare, CJ, LinkConnector, Pepperjam, Admitad, Clixgalore, and Commission Factory.

Do I need to create banners for my affiliate program?

Yes. Banners are the number one tool affiliates use to promote you. You need a variety of sizes and formats, updated regularly. The more options they have, the more affiliates will actually promote your program.

How do I decide what commission to offer?

Commission is one of the most important factors affiliates consider. To set yours, look at: - What your competitors are offering - Your profit margins - Your website’s conversion rate - Your brand’s strength - Your product prices

We’ll help you land on a number that attracts affiliates without killing your margins.

How do I pay for sales or leads?

Here’s the process: 1. All sales come to you for validation or cancellation (in case an order was canceled or never paid for). 2. You respond as soon as possible so affiliates know the status of their commissions. 3. At the end of each month, you receive a list of all validated sales. Those are the ones you pay commissions on.

You only pay for sales after you’ve collected money from the actual customer.

What is the tracking code I have to place on my website?

The affiliate network needs a tracking script on your checkout thank-you page to record sales. The script captures the order ID and amount for each transaction. Placement is simple, we’ll walk you through it.

What is a cookie and how do I determine its duration?

A cookie is a small file saved on a user’s computer when they click an affiliate link. It tracks their activity for a set period (the cookie duration).

Example: If your cookie duration is 30 days and someone clicks an affiliate link today but doesn’t buy until next week, that sale still counts. If they buy 32 days later, it doesn’t.

Longer cookie durations are more attractive to affiliates because they get credit for sales that happen later. We’ll help you set a duration that works.

How do I cancel this service?

All services are prepaid subscriptions. Depending on how much work has been completed at the time of cancellation, you may receive a full refund, partial refund, or no refund. Reach out to me directly through the dashboard or contact our support team to cancel.

Do you try to improve my visibility, or just track it?

This package just tracks. You’ll know where you stand. If you want active optimization (getting your business cited more often in AI results), that’s a different project.

Which service area should I pick?

We’re tracking whether your business gets mentioned when someone asks an AI assistant a question related to your service areas. For example, if you’re a plumber in Boise, we might track prompts like “best emergency plumber in Boise” or “who should I call for a water heater repair in Boise.” We run these prompts regularly and document if and how your business appears in the AI’s response.

Can you guarantee my business will show up in AI results?
No. This is a tracking service, not a manipulation service. We show you where you stand. What you do with that information is up to you, but most businesses use this data to inform their content strategy, review management, and overall online presence.

Why only 2 service areas?
Because meaningful tracking requires focus. We’d rather monitor 15 high-quality prompts across 2 core service areas than spread thin across 10 vague categories. If your business has more than 2 primary services, we can discuss custom tracking.

Multiple times per month, spread out to account for AI model updates and variations in responses. You get a consolidated report monthly.

What if my business never shows up?
Then you know exactly where the problem is. Most businesses that don’t appear in AI results have gaps in their online footprint, review presence, or content strategy. We can’t fix that with this tracking service alone, but we can tell you it’s happening so you can address it.

On-site SEO factors (page titles, meta descriptions, header structure, content quality, internal linking, site speed, mobile responsiveness), local search presence (Google Business Profile optimization, NAP consistency across directories, local pack rankings), technical issues (crawl errors, indexation problems, robots.txt, XML sitemap), and competitive benchmarking (how your metrics compare to businesses ranking above you).

How long does the audit take?

Typically 3-5 business days from the time you provide site access. You get a PDF report with findings and prioritized recommendations.

Do I need to give you site access?

Not required for the basic audit. If you want me to check Google Analytics, Search Console, or backend technical issues (server response times, database queries), then yes. Otherwise I can run the audit with public-facing data.

What happens after I get the report?

You decide. Some businesses use the audit to fix things in-house. Others hand it to their current web developer. Or we talk about working together on the fixes. There’s no obligation to buy additional services.

Is this audit specific to Boise or local businesses?

Yes. The competitive analysis looks at businesses ranking in Boise and Treasure Valley searches. The local visibility section checks your presence in Google’s local pack, Apple Maps, and citation directories that matter for Idaho businesses.

Can I get an audit for multiple locations?

Yes, but each location is a separate audit. If you have three locations, that’s three reports (one per location with localized competitive analysis).

What’s the difference between static and HTML5 banners?

Static banners are image files, PNG or JPG. One frame, no animation. HTML5 banners are code-based. They can animate, have clickable elements, adapt to screen size dynamically. HTML5 loads faster and scales better, but some older ad networks only accept static images. I can do either.

Yes. You own the files. Use them as much as you want, across any platform.

design__magento-ecommerce.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

Are your websites custom-made or do you use templates?

Everything is custom. No templates. We design and code your site from scratch so it fits your brand and works exactly how you need it to.

How long does it take to build a Magento site?

Usually 2-3 weeks. Depends on how complex the design is and how many revision rounds you want. If you have a specific launch date, tell me upfront and we’ll plan around it.

What technologies do you use?

Magento 2 core. HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, PHP. If you want a CMS for managing static pages (About Us, blog posts, etc.), we can integrate WordPress or another system. But the ecommerce side runs on Magento.

How does the development process work?

Six steps:

  1. Planning - We review your business, products, and goals. If you’re rebuilding an existing site, we audit what’s working and what’s not.
  2. Mockup Design - We design a homepage concept with your colors, fonts, layout, and image style.
  3. Design Revision - You review the mockup and request changes.
  4. Development - Once you approve the design, we build out the full site.
  5. Testing - We test checkout, mobile responsiveness, page speed, everything.
  6. Launch - Final approval, then we go live.

Do you provide web hosting?

Not included in the base package. This covers design, development, and 30 days of post-launch support. You can add hosting separately if you want. Most Magento stores need managed hosting anyway (shared hosting won’t cut it), so we’ll recommend a provider if you don’t have one.

What if I need more than one round of revisions?

One round is included. If you need more, we can do it, but it’ll add time and cost. Best approach: take your time on the first review and catch everything.

How should I supply content?

Text and images. If you can get that to us before we start the homepage mockup, it helps. We’ll use real content instead of placeholders, so the design reflects what the live site will actually look like.

Do I need all my content ready before we start?

No. We can use placeholder text and stock images to get the design moving. You can finalize content while we’re building.

What kind of support do you offer?

Email support during business hours. Usually respond within 24 hours, sometimes faster. The base package includes 30 days of post-launch support. After that, you can add a maintenance plan if you want ongoing help.

Are there hidden costs?

No. We quote based on what you tell us you need. If scope changes (you add features mid-project), we’ll let you know the cost before we do the extra work. Nothing gets added without your approval.

ppc__advertising-1-plan.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Google Display Network?

It’s Google’s network of over 2 million websites, apps, YouTube videos, and Gmail inboxes where display ads can appear. If you’ve ever seen a banner ad on a news site or a video ad on YouTube, that’s the Display Network. It reaches over 90% of internet users.

Display ads let you target people by interest, demographics, and behavior, not just search keywords. You’re reaching people while they browse, not just when they search for your product.

Who should use this?

Businesses that want visibility beyond search ads. If you’re launching a new product, promoting an event, or just want your brand in front of potential customers while they’re online, display works.

It’s also good for retargeting, showing ads to people who’ve already visited your site. (Retargeting setup is a separate add-on.)

What happens after I sign up?

I reach out within 1 business day to schedule a kickoff call. We’ll go over your business, your offer, and what you want the campaign to do. Then I build the campaign and launch it. You’ll start getting weekly status emails and see tasks logged on your dashboard in real time.

How much does this cost?

The service fee is separate from your ad spend. You’re paying me to set up and manage the campaign. You pay Google directly for the ads themselves (up to $500/month on this plan). So total monthly cost = service fee + ad spend.

How do I know it’s working?

ppc__advertising-2-plan.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Google Display Network?

The Google Display Network lets you reach people while they’re browsing websites, watching YouTube, checking Gmail, or using mobile apps. You can target by context (specific sites or content themes), audience (demographics, interests, shopping behavior), location, and more. The Display Network reaches over 90% of internet users worldwide.

Who should use this service?

Businesses that want to improve their online presence and show their products to people who are actively looking for them. Display ads increase your visibility and help you target the right customers at the right time.

How much does Display PPC cost?

We offer Display PPC in three packages to fit different needs and budgets. All packages include dedicated support and full transparency into the work being performed. Pricing is based on the monthly ad spend you’re managing. Higher-tier packages include more diverse activities.

How do I know if the campaign is working?

You’ll have access to your BMG dashboard, which shows every action we perform and lets you monitor overall PPC performance. We also send weekly and monthly updates so you can review campaign activity and make sure your goals are being met.

Will this guarantee sales and leads?

No. Like any advertising, Google Ads can’t guarantee sales or customers. Once your ads are live, we can’t control the decisions of potential customers. What we can do is target the right people at the right time. The rest is up to them.

Do I need a landing page, or can I just use my homepage?

A great landing page is critical for any PPC campaign. People clicking your ads have stronger purchase intent than general browsers, so the landing page needs to match their expectations. We offer free landing page analysis and can handle landing page optimization and setup for an additional fee.

Do I have access to the PPC campaign?

Yes. You can see campaign performance anytime through your BMG dashboard.

How do I cancel if I need to?

All services are prepaid on a subscription basis. Depending on the amount of work completed at the time of cancellation, you may receive a full refund, a partial refund, no refund, or owe additional fees. Reach out to me directly to cancel.

ppc__advertising-3-plan.faqs_html:

What is the Google Display Network?

It’s Google’s network of over 2 million websites, apps, and properties where your ads can appear. This includes YouTube, Gmail, news sites, blogs, and mobile apps.

Display Network reaches over 90% of internet users worldwide. I use targeting options to show your ads in the right contexts (specific sites, content categories), to the right audiences (demographics, interests, purchase intent), in the right locations.

Who should use this service?

Businesses that want to build brand awareness and reach customers before they’re actively searching. Works especially well if: - You have a visual product or service - You’re entering a new market - You want to support your search campaigns with top-of-funnel awareness - You’re running retargeting and need a bigger audience pool

What happens after I sign up?

We’ll schedule a kickoff call to go over your business, campaign goals, and target audience. I’ll ask about your best customers, geographic focus, and any placements you definitely want or want to avoid.

Then I build and launch the campaign. You’ll get weekly updates and monthly performance reports. I’m available anytime through your dashboard or direct contact.

How much does this cost?

This plan covers the campaign management and creative work. Your ad spend (up to $4,000/month) is separate and goes directly to Google. You control that budget and can adjust it anytime.

How do I know the campaign is working?

You’ll have full access to your BMG dashboard showing every placement, every click, every dollar spent. Real-time reporting, not month-end summaries.

I also send weekly status updates and monthly performance reports. You’ll see exactly which placements are driving traffic, which ads are getting clicks, and where your budget is going.

Does this guarantee sales?

No advertising can guarantee sales. I can put your ads in front of the right people at the right time, but I can’t control whether they buy.

What I can control: targeting accuracy, ad quality, placement optimization, and budget efficiency. The goal is to get your cost-per-click down and your click-through rate up. The rest depends on your offer and your site.

Should I use a landing page or just send traffic to my homepage?

Landing page. Always.

People clicking display ads have higher purchase intent than general browsers. They need a page that matches the ad and makes the next step obvious. Your homepage has too many distractions.

I offer landing page analysis and can recommend changes. If you need a new landing page built, that’s available as an add-on.

Do I have access to the campaign?

Yes. Full transparency. You can log into your dashboard anytime and see placements, keywords, bids, performance metrics - everything.

What if I need to cancel?

You can cancel anytime. Refund depends on how much work has been completed at the time of cancellation. Reach out directly and we’ll handle it.

Direct message through your dashboard, email, or phone. I’m your point of contact for the entire campaign.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is PPC?

Pay-per-click advertising puts your products in front of people searching for them on Google. You pay when someone clicks your ad and lands on your site. It’s a way to buy visits instead of waiting for organic traffic to build.

What is a Shopping Campaign?

Shopping campaigns show your products directly in Google search results with images, prices, and store info. People see what you’re selling before they click. You only pay when they click through to your site or check your local inventory.

What are Shopping Ads?

Shopping ads include a product photo, title, price, and store name. They’re more visual than text ads. They show up when people search for products like yours, which means you’re reaching buyers who are already looking.

Who should use this service?

Businesses that want to show their products to people actively searching for them on Google. If you’re selling online and want to reach customers at the moment they’re ready to buy, this works.

What happens after I sign up?

I’ll reach out within a day to schedule a kickoff call. We’ll talk through your goals, what you’re selling, and who you’re targeting. Then I launch the campaign and send weekly and monthly updates as we go.

How much does Ecommerce PPC cost?

We offer three packages based on ad spend and scope. Pricing is monthly. The higher-tier packages include more work, but every package includes support and full transparency into what’s being done. Check the pricing sheet for details on what’s in each tier.

How do I know PPC is working?

You’ll have access to a dashboard showing campaign performance in real time. You can also log into the Google Ads account directly if you want to dig into the data yourself. Weekly and monthly reports break down what’s happening and whether we’re hitting your goals.

Will my ad always show up at the top of Google?

No, and you wouldn’t want it to. Google ranks ads based on over 100 factors including bid price, quality score, click volume, and relevance. That system lets smaller advertisers compete with bigger budgets. The goal is to show up when it matters, not burn through your budget showing up everywhere.

Does this guarantee sales?

No form of advertising guarantees sales. What we can do is put your products in front of the right people at the right time. After that, it’s on them whether they buy. PPC gets you visibility with people who are already looking for what you sell.

Can I just send traffic to my homepage, or do I need a landing page?

A good landing page makes a huge difference. People clicking PPC ads are closer to buying than general browsers, so the page they land on needs to match their intent. I can analyze your current pages for free and set up optimized landing pages for an additional fee if needed.

Do I have access to the campaign you create?

Yes. You’ll see performance data on the dashboard anytime you want. Full transparency.

How do I cancel?

Services are prepaid monthly. Depending on how much work has been completed when you cancel, you may get a full refund, partial refund, or no refund. Reach out through the dashboard or contact me directly to cancel.

ppc__local-search-ads.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What’s the difference between Local Service Ads and regular Google Ads?

Local Service Ads show up above everything else on Google search results. You get the Google Guaranteed badge. You pay per lead, not per click. Regular Google Ads are below the Local Service Ads section and you pay per click whether or not the person contacts you.

What are Google’s eligibility requirements?

You need to pass a background check, verify your business license, show proof of insurance, and meet Google’s requirements for your specific service category. We help you through the process.

How long does it take to get the Google Guaranteed badge?

Usually 5-10 business days once you submit all required documentation. Sometimes longer if Google needs additional verification.

What kind of businesses qualify for Local Service Ads?

Google currently offers Local Service Ads for home services, professional services, and a few other local service categories. Plumbers, electricians, HVAC, locksmiths, lawyers, real estate agents, and others. If you’re not sure, ask.

How do I know if the leads are any good?

You get all the contact details through your Local Service Ad dashboard. You can dispute any lead that doesn’t meet quality standards and Google will refund it.

Can I just use my homepage instead of a landing page?

Frequently Asked Questions

What is retargeting?

Someone visits your website. They look around, maybe read a service page, then leave without calling or filling out a form. Retargeting puts an ad in front of them later, while they’re on a news site, watching a YouTube video, checking their Gmail. It keeps you in front of people who already showed interest.

Who should use this?

Businesses that get traffic but don’t convert all of it on the first visit. If you’re spending money on ads or SEO and people are visiting your site, retargeting helps you get a second (and third) shot at those visitors.

What happens after I sign up?

We’ll schedule a kickoff call to go over your business, what you’re selling, and who you want to reach. Then we build out the campaign, install the tracking tag, create the ads, set up the targeting. You’ll start seeing weekly reports as soon as the campaign is live.

How much does this cost?

Pricing is listed on this page. This is the mid-tier retargeting package. If you’re spending less than $800/month on ads, the Retargeting 1 package might be a better fit. If you’re over $1,200/month, look at Retargeting 3.

How do I know it’s working?

You’ll have access to your BMG dashboard where you can see every ad we’ve created, every placement adjustment, and all the performance metrics, impressions, clicks, conversions. We also send weekly and monthly reports. You’ll know exactly where your ad dollars are going and what they’re producing.

Does this guarantee sales?

No. Retargeting gets your ad in front of people who already visited your site. It increases the odds they come back and convert. But it’s still advertising, we can’t control whether someone picks up the phone or fills out a form. What we can do is make sure the right people see your ad at the right time.

Can I just send people to my homepage?

You can, but a dedicated landing page almost always works better. People clicking a retargeting ad are warmer than cold traffic, they’ve already seen your site. A landing page that matches the ad and speaks directly to what they were looking at will convert better than a generic homepage. We offer landing page setup and optimization as an add-on if you need it.

Do I get access to the campaign?

Yes. You’ll see everything on your dashboard, ad performance, placement data, spend, conversions. Full transparency.

How do I cancel?

Email me or reach out through the dashboard. Depending on how much work has been completed in the current billing cycle, you may get a full refund, partial refund, or no refund. We’ll walk you through it.

Who should use this?

Anyone with a considered purchase. If people don’t buy on the first visit, you need this. Works especially well for B2B services, high-ticket products, anything with a longer sales cycle.

What happens after I sign up?

ppc__traffic-plan.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is PPC?

Pay Per Click. You pay Google (or Bing) to show your ad when someone searches for keywords you’re targeting. You only pay when someone clicks. It’s the fastest way to get traffic to your site, because you’re buying placement instead of earning it through SEO.

Who should use this service?

Businesses that need visibility right now. If you’re not ranking organically yet, or if your competitors are outbidding you for attention, this gets you in the game. Works best for service businesses, local shops, and anyone with a clear offer and a phone number or contact form.

What happens after I sign up?

We’ll schedule a kick-off call within 24 hours. I’ll ask about your business, your goals, your target customer. Then I’ll get to work on keyword research and campaign setup. You’ll see the first activity in your dashboard within a few days, and the campaign goes live as soon as everything’s wired up.

How much does PPC cost?

This plan is $158/mo for the service. Your ad spend (up to $1500/mo) is separate, that goes directly to Google. No setup fees. You can add call tracking for $19/mo if you want to track which calls came from which ads.

How do I know it’s working?

You’ll have access to the dashboard where you can see clicks, conversions, cost per click, cost per lead, everything in real time. I also send weekly and monthly reports that break down what’s happening and what I’m adjusting. Plus, you’ll have login credentials to the Google Ads account itself, so you can poke around anytime.

Does this guarantee my ad shows up at the top of Google every time?

No. Google ranks ads based on a bunch of factors, your bid, your quality score, your click-through rate, how relevant your landing page is. Higher bids help, but they’re not the only thing. The system is designed so smaller advertisers can compete with bigger budgets if their ads are more relevant. That’s good for you.

Will this guarantee sales?

No. PPC gets people to your site. What happens after that is on you, your offer, your landing page, your follow-up. I can drive traffic. I can’t make people buy. But I can make sure the traffic is relevant, so the people clicking are actually looking for what you sell.

Should I use a landing page or just send people to my homepage?

Landing page, 100%. People clicking your ad have high intent. They’re looking for something specific. Your homepage is too general. A landing page that matches the ad and has one clear call to action will convert way better. I can analyze your current pages for free and recommend what needs to change. If you want me to build or optimize a landing page, that’s an add-on.

Do I have access to the campaign?

Yes. You get login credentials to the Google Ads account and full access to the dashboard. You can see everything I’m doing in real time.

How do I cancel?

Email me or message through the dashboard. All services are prepaid monthly. Depending on how much work has been done that month, you might get a full refund, partial refund, or no refund. I’ll walk you through it if you need to cancel.

ppc__ultimate-plan.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is PPC?
Pay-per-click advertising. You pay when someone clicks your ad on Google or Bing. It’s a way to show up in search results without waiting for organic SEO to kick in. You’re buying visibility for people actively searching for what you offer.

Who should use this service?
Businesses that want immediate search visibility and are ready to invest in paid traffic. If you’ve got a product or service people are searching for, PPC puts you in front of them right when they’re looking.

What happens after I sign up?
I’ll reach out within 24 hours to schedule a campaign kick-off call. We’ll discuss your objectives, account strategy, and what success looks like. Then we launch the campaign and send you weekly and monthly updates.

How much does PPC cost?
We offer five packages based on your monthly ad spend. Pricing is monthly with no onboarding fees. Higher tiers include more activities, but every package includes dedicated support and full transparency into the work.

How do I know it’s working?
You get full access to your BMG dashboard showing every action performed and every result tracked. You’ll also have direct login access to your Google Ads account. Weekly and monthly reports keep you updated on performance and progress toward your goals.

Will my ad always be on the top of Google?
No, and in most cases you wouldn’t want it to be. Google ranks ads based on over 100 factors, bid price, quality score, click volume, content relevance. This system lets smaller advertisers compete with bigger budgets by rewarding ad quality, not just spend.

Will it guarantee sales and leads?
No. PPC is advertising, not a guarantee. We target the right people at the right time with the right message. Once they click through, the decision to buy is theirs. Our job is to get qualified traffic to your site.

Should I use a landing page or just send traffic to my homepage?
Landing pages almost always perform better. People clicking PPC ads have purchase intent. A dedicated landing page optimized for that intent converts better than a general homepage. We offer landing page analysis and can build optimized pages for an additional fee.

Do I have access to the campaign you create?
Yes. Full transparency. You can log into your Google Ads account anytime and see exactly what we’re doing. Your BMG dashboard shows performance data in real time.

How do I cancel?
Reach out to me directly or contact support through the dashboard. Services are prepaid monthly. Depending on work completed at cancellation, you may receive a full refund, partial refund, or owe additional fees for work in progress.

Who do I contact if I have questions?
Me. I’m your point of contact. Reach me through the dashboard, email, or phone. For urgent PPC issues, support is available through the dashboard conversation tool.

What is YouTube advertising / PPC?

Pay-per-click advertising on YouTube means you pay when someone watches your video ad or clicks through to your site. It’s part of Google Ads. You’re buying visibility in front of people who are already watching videos related to your business.

Who should use this?

Businesses that have video content and want to reach people on YouTube specifically. If your audience is on YouTube and you have videos that explain or showcase what you do, this gets those videos in front of the right people.

Does this guarantee sales?

No. Ads put you in front of people. They don’t force anyone to buy. I can target the right audience and optimize for better performance, but the rest is up to how well your video and offer connect with viewers.

Can I just send people to my homepage?

You can, but it’s not ideal. A dedicated landing page that matches the ad message converts better. If you don’t have one, we can talk about building that separately.

Do I get access to the Google Ads account?

You’ll see all performance data through your BMG dashboard. Campaign stats, spend, views, conversions, it’s all there. The Google Ads account itself stays under my agency billing setup, but you have full visibility into what’s happening.

How do I cancel if I need to?

What’s the difference between local SEO and national SEO?

Local SEO targets people within a few miles of your business location. It’s built to drive foot traffic to a physical location. We use geo-centric keywords, locally relevant content, and citations on local directories.

National SEO is location-neutral. Good for e-commerce or nationwide campaigns where your customers are spread out.

Does SEO work for every type of business?

Yes, as long as you offer a viable product or service. SEO can move your website higher in search rankings and drive more sales.

How long does it take to see results?

SEO takes time. Most customers start seeing positive momentum in 3-4 months. Many reach the first page of Google after 6 months if conditions are right.

How do I know the SEO is working?

seo__national-value.faqs_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

What happens after I place an order?

We get started. You’ll hear from me within 24 hours to gather information and kick off the campaign. Once we launch on the dashboard, you’ll see deliverables rolling in and get weekly and monthly updates on progress.

What’s the difference between Local SEO and National SEO?

Local SEO targets customers within a few miles of your business. It’s for brick-and-mortar locations that need foot traffic. We use geo-specific keywords, local content, and local directories.

National SEO is location-neutral. It’s for businesses that sell online or need visibility across a wider area. If you’re running an e-commerce site or serving customers nationwide, this is the right approach.

Does SEO work for every type of business?

Yes. If you have a viable product or service, SEO can move your website higher in search rankings and drive more sales.

What countries do you work in?

We provide SEO services for businesses across the globe. Supported languages are English (North America, UK, Australia) and Spanish.

How long does it take to see results?

SEO takes time. Most customers start to see positive momentum in 3 to 4 months. Many small businesses reach the first page of Google after 6 months if the right conditions are met.

What tactics do you use to improve website rank?

Keyword Optimization: We research the best keywords for your business.

Website Optimization: We clean up and upgrade your website code and user experience.

Business Profile Development: We create and maintain profiles on trusted online business directories.

Link Portfolio Development: We create a diverse portfolio of backlinks and place them on industry sites, news sites, blogs, and articles.

Custom Content Creation: We create and post high-quality content to keep your website fresh and at the top of search rankings.

Service and Performance Reporting: We provide reports that show how your campaign is performing and all the work we’ve done.

Campaign Monitoring: We actively monitor every SEO campaign to identify areas for improvement. We also provide monthly consultations to review progress and ensure goals are being met.

How are the keywords chosen?

The keywords we suggest are unique to your business. We consider your specific products or services, industry, location, competition, and more. The goal is to find the keywords that will provide the most impact.

How much does this cost?

Pricing is displayed on this page. We offer different packages to fit different needs and budgets. All packages include dedicated support and full transparency into the work being performed.

How do I know the SEO is working?

You get full transparency. You’ll have access to your BMG dashboard, which shows every SEO action being performed on and off your website. You can track every action, monitor keyword movement, and see overall performance. We also provide weekly and monthly updates.

How do I cancel?

All services are on a prepaid subscription basis. Depending on the work completed at the time of cancellation, you may receive a full refund, a partial refund, no refund, or owe additional fees. To cancel, reach out to me directly.
